behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all required files (component.ts, scheduler.ts, AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/handle.md), the 'infinite loop detected' literal, the Promise<AbortSignal> return type, and the remove() cleanup behavior.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes expected behavior without dictating specific variable names or line changes; the agent must still design the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific internal names like 'MAX_CASCADING_UPDATES', 'cascadingUpdateCount', and 'renderCtrl' — a correct alternative implementation using different identifiers (e.g., MAX_FLUSH_DEPTH, depthCounter, renderController) would fail despite matching the required behavior.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute subprocesses (tsc, pnpm build/lint) and use a TypeScript compile check for the Promise<AbortSignal> signature, though several are text grep checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The typecheck test requires actual API signature change; structural checks require specific implementation markers; build/lint tests require working code.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Promise API, cascading guard, renderCtrl cleanup, and documentation updates are all tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with header comments separating pass-to-pass, fail-to-pass behavioral, structural, and documentation checks.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names appear consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to files but doesn't name specific buggy lines; file localization is the stated scope.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Not an agentmd-edits-only task; code changes are primary.
